classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

title: Even the Soul of a Fly is More Excellent Than the Light.

source.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

verses.book: On Two Souls, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: And here, if perchance in their confusion they had inquired of me whether I thought that the soul even of a fly surpasses that light, I should have replied, yes, nor should it have troubled me that the fly is little, but it should have confirmed me that it is alive.

verses.book: On Two Souls, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: For it is inquired, what causes those members so diminutive to grow, what leads so minute a body here and there according to its natural appetite, what moves its feet in numerical order when it is running, what regulates and gives vibration to its wings when flying?

verses.book: On Two Souls, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: This thing whatever it is in so small a creature towers up so prominently to one well considering, that it excels any lightning flashing upon the eyes.
